title: Add HorizontalPodAutoscaler sub-reconciler
summary: Adds optional spec.autoscaling field to the Keystone CRD with AutoscalingSpec struct (MinReplicas, MaxReplicas, TargetCPUUtilization, TargetMemoryUtilization), CEL XValidation requiring at least one metric target, and kubebuilder validation markers. Implements reconcileHPA sub-reconciler with three lifecycle paths (autoscaling nil → delete/skip, autoscaling set → ensure), EnsureHPA/DeleteHPA shared helpers following EnsurePDB pattern, HPAReady condition with ObservedGeneration, RBAC markers, Owns() watch, defense-in-depth webhook validation, and comprehensive unit/integration tests. Documentation updated for both CRD reference and reconciler architecture.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist for EnsureHPA (create, update, idempotent, ownerRef reconciliation, label merge, annotation merge)
Unit tests exist for DeleteHPA (deletes, noop when absent)
Unit tests exist for reconcileHPA (all three lifecycle paths)
Unit tests exist for buildKeystoneHPA (scaleTargetRef, labels, minReplicas default, explicit minReplicas, maxReplicas, CPU only, memory only, both metrics, pointer independence)
Error propagation tests (ensure error, delete error with interceptors)
ObservedGeneration tested for both enabled and not-required paths
Webhook validation tests for autoscaling (valid CPU/memory/both, invalid no targets, maxReplicas zero, min exceeds max, nil is valid)
Integration tests updated (FullReconcile_Brownfield, FullReconcile_Managed, ConditionProgression, EarlyReturn scenarios)
Controller orchestration tests updated (SetsAllSubConditionsTrue, AggregateReady, EarlyReturn scenarios)

code_quality_checklist.item: EnsureHPA follows EnsurePDB pattern exactly (Get+IsNotFound+Create, DeepCopy snapshot, ownerRef, label/annotation merge, spec update, apiequality guard)
reconcileHPA correctly implements all three lifecycle paths
HPAReady condition uses correct reasons (HPAReady vs HPANotRequired) with ObservedGeneration
buildKeystoneHPA produces correct scaleTargetRef and conditional metrics
Error messages wrap with descriptive context
No dead code, no magic numbers, clear naming
Functions are focused and small (reconcileHPA: 30 lines, buildKeystoneHPA: 53 lines, EnsureHPA: 60 lines)
SPDX headers present on new files

security_checklist.item: RBAC marker includes all required verbs (get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete) for autoscaling group
No hardcoded secrets or sensitive data
Input validated via kubebuilder markers, CEL XValidation, and webhook defense-in-depth

architecture_checklist.item: HPAReady added to subConditionTypes in correct position (after DeploymentReady, before BootstrapReady)
reconcileHPA placed after reconcileDeployment in reconcile chain
HPA name follows {name}-api convention matching Deployment name
DeleteHPA is idempotent (no-op when HPA absent)
AutoscalingSpec uses pointer-for-optional pattern matching FederationSpec
DeepCopy generated correctly for AutoscalingSpec (all pointer fields handled)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: EnsureHPA reuses established EnsurePDB pattern from shared library

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors from EnsureHPA and DeleteHPA propagated immediately
Condition set before returning on both success paths

defensive_checklist.item: CEL XValidation enforces at least one metric target at CRD admission
Webhook defense-in-depth validates maxReplicas >= 1, minReplicas >= 1, minReplicas <= maxReplicas
Webhook defense-in-depth validates at least one metric target
Defense-in-depth bounds checks for TargetCPUUtilization (1-100) and TargetMemoryUtilization (1-100)

issues_found.description: Missing defense-in-depth bounds checks for TargetCPUUtilization and TargetMemoryUtilization. Both fields carry +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1 and +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=100 markers, but the webhook validate() function has no corresponding runtime guards. In the same code block, MinReplicas (*int32, Minimum=1) and MaxReplicas (int32, Minimum=1) both have runtime guards. This inconsistency matches the external review pattern 'Apply defense-in-depth checks consistently to all similarly-marked fields' (observed in CC-0011). If CRD admission is bypassed (direct etcd write, admission misconfiguration), values like 0 or 150 would be accepted for utilization targets.
issues_found.evidence:
Lines 207-227 guard MinReplicas (Minimum=1) and MaxReplicas (Minimum=1) but lines 228-233 only check that at least one metric is non-nil — no range validation for the metric values themselves:
```go
if k.Spec.Autoscaling.MaxReplicas < 1 {  // guarded
...
if k.Spec.Autoscaling.MinReplicas != nil && *k.Spec.Autoscaling.MinReplicas < 1 {  // guarded
...
if k.Spec.Autoscaling.TargetCPUUtilization == nil && k.Spec.Autoscaling.TargetMemoryUtilization == nil {  // only nil check, no range
```
Compare with keystone_types.go:112-120:
```go
// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=100
TargetCPUUtilization *int32
// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=100
TargetMemoryUtilization *int32
```

issues_found.description: TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations does not include autoscaling validation. Per the external review pattern 'Ensure test names accurately describe their coverage scope', this test claims to exercise all validation paths simultaneously but omits autoscaling. All other validation domains (replicas, fernet, credentialKeys, plugins, policyOverrides, cache, database) are represented.
issues_found.fix:
Add an autoscaling violation to the test input and assert its presence in the error message:
```go
// REQ-001 (CC-0038): Break autoscaling — set with no metric targets.
k.Spec.Autoscaling = &AutoscalingSpec{
    MaxReplicas: 5,
}
```
And add assertion:
```go
g.Expect(errMsg).To(ContainSubstring("autoscaling"))
```
issues_found.evidence:
Lines 715-731 set invalid values for replicas, fernet, credentialKeys, plugins, policyOverrides, cache, and database — but k.Spec.Autoscaling is never set. Lines 736-743 assert all domains except autoscaling:
```go
g.Expect(errMsg).To(ContainSubstring("replicas"))
g.Expect(errMsg).To(ContainSubstring("maxActiveKeys"))
...
g.Expect(errMsg).To(ContainSubstring("database"))
g.Expect(errMsg).To(ContainSubstring("credentialKeys"))
// no autoscaling assertion
```

next_steps: Add defense-in-depth bounds checks for TargetCPUUtilization and TargetMemoryUtilization in the webhook validate() function with corresponding unit tests
Update TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations to include autoscaling violation and assertion
